item.title: Relief Sweeps Markets After Two-Week Ceasefire Deal
item.description: Oil plunges and traders revive bets on interest rate cuts from the Fed.

item.title: Finland Is Working Toward IPO of Gambling Company Veikkaus
item.description: Finland is taking step toward potentially listing state-owned gambling company Veikkaus Oy with several milestones still to be met before it’s ready for public markets.

item.title: Amundi Scooped Up Stocks When Most Investors Rushed for the Exit
item.description: Amundi SA has been buying into the selloff in equity markets just as most investors were slashing their stock exposure on fears of an escalation in the Iran war. It paid off.

item.title: South Africa Leads Rebound in Emerging Markets Hit By Iran Conflict
item.description: South Africa’s rand soared, government bond yields tumbled and stocks jumped the most in six years as investors piled back into emerging-market assets that were worst-hit by the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Argentines Plunge Into Debt for Bills, Cars and Birthday Parties
item.description: Cristian Dezilio thought the loan he took out to buy his son a used car to get around Buenos Aires’ suburbs was plenty manageable at $355 a month in pesos.

item.title: Samsung Holder Seeks to Sell Up to $2.1 Billion in Block Trade
item.description: A Samsung Electronics Co. shareholder is seeking to sell all their remaining shares in the company for up to 3.1 trillion won ($2.1 billion) through a block trade, according to terms of the deal seen by Bloomberg News, in one of the biggest such offerings in South Korea.

item.title: China Trumpets ‘Own Efforts’ in Pushing For US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: A Chinese diplomat said Beijing had made its “own efforts” in pushing for a ceasefire between the US and Iran, shortly after Donald Trump credited China with playing a pivotal role in that deal.

item.title: White House Economists Say Stablecoin Rewards Won’t Harm Banks
item.description: Banning crypto firms from offering customers yield on stablecoins would not have a meaningful effect on community banks, White House economists said in a report on Wednesday — marking the latest development in a fierce conflict between the two industries that has stalled legislation in Congress.

item.title: Japan, Australia Warn of Asia Security Vacuum With Focus on Iran
item.description: North Korea’s latest missile launch highlights the security challenges existing in the Indo-Pacific region as the world focuses on the tensions in the Middle East, according to the defense ministers of Japan and Australia.

item.title: Japan Taps Smaller Ships to Get US Oil Faster on Supply Concerns
item.description: Some Japanese refiners are seeking to speed up deliveries of US crude following disruptions caused by the Iran war, opting for smaller ships that can transit the Panama Canal rather than sail around Africa.

item.title: Simkus Says Situation Moving Toward ECB’s Adverse Scenario
item.description: The Iran war is pushing the euro-area economy closer to the European Central Bank’s adverse scenario, Governing Council member Gediminas Simkus said.

item.title: Big Funds Pile Into Treasuries, AI Stocks as Iran War Risks Fade
item.description: Some of the world’s largest investment firms are buying bonds and artificial-intelligence stocks while selling the dollar, betting that war-driven uncertainty has passed its peak following the US-Iran ceasefire.

item.title: Malaysia Importers Use Ceasefire Window to Buy Dollar, Citi Says
item.description: Some Malaysian importers snapped up dollars on Wednesday, capitalizing on the greenback’s retreat, according to Citigroup Inc.

item.title: European Bonds Jump as Energy Prices Drop on Iran Ceasefire
item.description: German and UK bonds surged after energy prices sank following a ceasefire between the US and Iran, prompting traders to slash bets on rate hikes this year.

item.title: Telenor Faces Lawsuit in Norway Over Data Protection in Myanmar
item.description: Telenor ASA is facing a lawsuit in Norway over sharing data with authorities in Myanmar from its local customers who were suspected of opposing the 2021 coup.

item.title: UK Builders Hit by Record Pickup in Cost Inflation, PMI Shows
item.description: British builders suffered the sharpest acceleration in cost pressures in at least three decades in March as the war in Iran drove up prices for fuel and raw materials.

item.title: China’s Top Envoy to Visit North Korea for First Time Since 2019
item.description: Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi will visit North Korea this week, marking his first trip to the neighboring country in more than six years as Beijing and Pyongyang look to strengthen their ties amid rising geopolitical uncertainty.

item.title: Hong Kong Mortgage Corp Weighs World’s Biggest Digital Bond Sale
item.description: Hong Kong Mortgage Corp. is considering raising up to HK$12 billion (US$1.5 billion) through its first digital bond sale, according to people familiar with the matter, in what may turn out to be the biggest-ever such offering globally.

item.title: Greece to Impose Social Media Ban for Children Under 15
item.description: Greece will ban access to social media for children under 15, Prime Minister Kyriakos Mitsotakis said.

item.title: Hesai, Taikang Life Are Said to Plan Investing in Manycore Tech’s Hong Kong IPO
item.description: Hesai Group and Taikang Life Insurance Co. are among key companies set to invest in spatial-design software maker Manycore Tech Inc.’s initial public offering in Hong Kong, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: China Allows Teapot Refiners More Crude to Cope With Iran Crunch
item.description: China has granted independent refiners additional crude import quotas to allow them to continue to produce fuels at officially mandated levels, helping Beijing to cope with the impact of disrupted supplies from the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Gilts Surge on Lower Oil and Gas Prices After US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: UK government bonds jumped after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire in exchange for Tehran reopening the Strait of Hormuz, triggering a sharp drop in energy prices.

item.title: Monte Paschi Fires CEO Lovaglio After Previously Ousting Him
item.description: Banca Monte dei Paschi di Siena SpA formally removed ousted Chief Executive Officer Luigi Lovaglio after it had stripped him of his powers for defying the board’s plan to replace him.

item.title: Dubai Stocks Soar Most in a Decade on Iran War Ceasefire Relief
item.description: Dubai’s benchmark stock index surged the most in more than a decade after the US and Iran agreed to a two-week ceasefire in a conflict that had rattled Gulf markets and threatened energy infrastructure across the region.

item.title: RBI Governor Says Forex Market Curbs Won’t Remain Forever
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India’s recent currency market curbs aimed at quelling speculation against the rupee are temporary and will not remain in place forever, Governor Sanjay Malhotra said.

item.title: China’s LNG Demand Won’t Bounce Back From Middle East Turmoil
item.description: Hopes for a strong rebound in China’s demand for liquefied natural gas are fading, despite the ceasefire called in the Middle East, as analysts caution over lingering supply risks and higher prices.

item.title: Malaysia ‘Corporate Mafia’ Feud Takes New Turn as Mogul Sued
item.description: Malaysian businessman Victor Chin is suing labor tycoon Aminul Islam for the “attempted hijacking” of a company, the latest development in the so-called corporate mafia case that’s drawn public attention in the Southeast Asian nation.

item.title: German Bonds Jump as Energy Prices Slump on US-Iran Ceasefire
item.description: German bonds and their euro area peers surged after energy prices sank following a ceasefire between the US and Iran.

item.title: E&P of Ghana Wins Bid to Run Damang Mine After Gold Fields Exit
item.description: Engineers and Planners Co., a closely held company headed by the brother of Ghana’s president, won a bid to take over the Damang gold mine after Gold Fields Ltd.’s transfers the asset to the government.

item.title: Iran Ceasefire Fuels Hope of Cheaper Funding for Japanese Firms
item.description: A bond market rally triggered by the US-Iran ceasefire has fueled hopes for cheaper borrowing at least for one Japanese bond issuer.
